metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5225
metadata.comment:
Rationale: "In control human brains without PD, Sept4 was abundant in presynaptic terminals of DA neurons projecting from SNpc to the striatum (putamen). Sept4 was colocalized in the varicosities with the dopamine transporter (DAT in Figures 1E-1G) and α-synuclein (αS in Figures 1H-1J). These data suggest physiological association of Sept4 with these presynaptic molecules in DA nerve terminals."

"As seen in the human brain, Sept4, α-synuclein, and DAT were abundant and colocalized in the axon terminals, especially at the varicosities (Figures 2F-2K)."

"To determine the molecular mechanism underlying the hypo-dopaminergic phenotype of Sept4-/- mice, we immunoprecipitated striatal homogenates of Sept4+/+ mice with anti-Sept4 antibodies, then immunoblotted for possible Sept4-interacting molecules. Consistent with our previous report (Ihara et al., 2003) and the colocalization data (Figures 1G and 1J and Figures 2H and 2K), Sept4 was coimmunoprecipitated with DAT and α-synuclein (Figures 6A and 6B).

Since Sept5, the closest paralog of Sept4, has been implicated in syntaxin (Stx)-mediated exocytosis (Beites et al., 1999), we tested the interaction between these septins and Stx-1A. Sept5 and Stx-1A were coimmunoprecipitated from the cerebellar cortex, but not from the striatum (data not shown). In contrast, Sept4 and Stx-1A were reciprocally coimmunoprecipitated from the striatum (Figures 6A and 6C).". Experimental description: M&M: "Double-labeling immunofluorescence studies were performed by incubating human and mouse tissue sections with anti-Sept4, -DAT, or -α-synuclein antibodies."

antibody:
"Information on the generation of Sept4 antibodies, including H5N and H5C2, has been published (Kinoshita et al., 1998, 2000; Ihara et al., 2003, 2005)."
"We raised guinea pig antibodies (H5M) against residues 56-307 of mouse Sept454kDa, and rabbit antibodies against recombinant polypeptide of the mouse dopamine type 2 receptor (Hagiwara et al., unpublished)"

"The authenticity and specificity of these antibodies were confirmed by immunoblot on recombinant proteins, by immunohistochemistry with antigen-adsorption, and with Sept4-/- tissue samples."
